Nvidia just dropped something that changes everything about how AI agents work. It's called Neatron 3 Super. It has a 1 million token context window. That means you can feed it your entire business, your docs, your emails, your code, your SLBs, and it reasons across all of it in one single prompt. No hitting limits, no forgetting context, no starting over. It's 2.2 times faster than the comparable models. It's fully open weight, so you can download it and run it yourself. is built specifically for AI agents, not chat bots, not assistants, actual agents that plan, execute, and get stuff done. And in this video, I'm going to show you exactly what it is, why it matters, and how to use it to build real AI systems right now. Stay with me because this one is big. Hey, if we haven't met already, I'm the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of SEO agency Goldie Agency. Whilst he's helping clients get more leads and customers, I'm here to help you get the latest AI updates. Julian Goldie reads every comment. So, make sure you comment below. Today, we're going deep on Neatron 3 Super. I'm going to give you everything you need, what it is, how it works, and how to use it to build real automation systems. Let's get into it. Nvidia just released a new openweight large language model called Nematron 3 Super. And this one is genuinely different from what we've seen before. Here are the core specs. 120 billion total parameters, but it only activates 12 billion at a time. That's because it uses something called mixture of experts, which is basically like having a team of specialists where only the right ones get called in for each task. The result is fast inference, lower compute cost, and strong reasoning quality all at the same time. The context window is 1 million tokens. This is the big one. Most models cap out at 128k or 200k tokens. Neatron 3 is at 1 million. That means you can load in an entire code base, an entire archive of documents, a full year of email history, and the model can reason across all of it in a single prompt without losing the thread. It also uses a hybrid architecture called Mamba Transformer. The Mamba layers handle long sequences efficiently. The transformer layers handle deep reasoning and retrieval. Together, they give you the speed of a lightweight model and the intelligence of a heavyweight one. On top of that, it uses multi-token prediction, meaning it generates multiple tokens at once instead of one at a time. That's where the speed gains come from. It's 2.2 times faster than comparable open models. For anyone running automated pipelines, that speed matters a lot. And the whole thing is fully open. Open weights, open data sets, open training recipes. You can download it, run it locally, or access it via API. That combination of openness, speed, and scale makes this one of the most capable models available for building agent systems. Right now, most AI models are great for answering questions. You ask, it answers done. But that's not how real automation works. Real workflows have many steps. They need context from the beginning of a process to make good decisions at the end. They need to hold a lot of information in mind at once, and they need to be fast enough that running them at scale doesn't become expensive or slow. That's exactly what Neatron 3 is designed for. The 1 million token context window means an agent can hold an entire business process in memory. The mixture of experts architecture means it stays efficient even at scale. The speed gains from multi-token prediction mean you can run more steps in less time. This isn't a chatbot upgrade. This is infrastructure for AI agents. Before we get into the real examples, I want to tell you about the AI profit boardroom. If you're serious about using AI to grow your business and you want to go beyond just playing with tools, the AI profit boardroom is where we build real systems. We cover automation workflows, agent pipelines, content systems, and everything else that actually moves the needle. We use AI to grow the boardroom itself. So everything we teach is tested and real. No fluff, no theory, just working systems you can apply immediately. Link is in the description. Go check it out. Now, let's keep going. Let me show you four real use cases. And for each one, I'm going to use the AI profit boardroom as the example because that's how you should think about this. Apply it to your actual business. The first one is an AI research agent. Imagine you want to keep the AI profit boardroom communityfed with fresh, relevant content every week. You give Neatron 3 a prompt that says, "Search for the top trending AI automation topics. Summarize the key points. Explain why each one matters for business owners. and suggest a video angle for the boardroom audience. Because of the 1 million token window, you can also include all your past video transcripts, your community FAQs, and your audience research. And the model tailors every output to your exact audience. One prompt, fully personalized research report. The second one is a content repurposing pipeline. You drop in a YouTube video transcript. You tell the model your brand voice. You say, "Produce a Twitter thread, a newsletter section, a LinkedIn post, and a short form video script from this one piece of content." For the AI profit boardroom, this means every video we post becomes five pieces of content automatically. One input, five outputs, zero extra effort. The third one is a coding agent. Say you want to build a custom onboarding flow for new members joining the boardroom. Something that asks them about their goals and recommends the right tools and resources from our library. You paste in your full code base. You describe what you want, and Neatron 3 reads the whole thing, understands the structure, and generates the new feature with full context. No more breaking things because the model forgot what your code looked like three steps ago. The fourth one is a full agent chain. This is where it gets interesting. Step one, a research agent finds the trending AI topics. Step two, a script agent writes the video. Step three, a content agent repurposes it across platforms. Step four, a scheduling agent plans the calendar. All automated, all connected, all running while you focus on other things. This is what multi- aent systems actually look like in practice. And Neatron 3 is built to power exactly this kind of pipeline. There are three ways to get started. The easiest is open router. Sign up, find the model listed as Nvidia Neatron 3 Super 120B A12B, and start making API calls. This is the fastest way to test it without any setup. The second option is hugging face where the full weights are available for local deployment if you have the hardware for it. The third is Nvidia NM and Nemo for enterprisegrade deployments at scale. For most people watching this, start with open router. It takes about 5 minutes to get your first call running. Here is a prompt you can copy and paste directly into Neatron 3 via Open Router today. You are an AI automation system for the AI profit boardroom. Your goal is to help grow a community focused on AI automation. Step one, identify the top three pain points of online business owners who want to use AI but don't know where to start. Step two, write three short, punchy video hooks that target each painoint. Step three, suggest one AI tool or workflow that solves each problem. Step four, write a three email nurture sequence that moves someone from curious to committed. Output everything clearly labeled and ready to use. That one prompt with Neatron's context window and reasoning ability gives you a full content and outreach strategy in minutes. That's not a chatbot. That's an agent doing real work. All right, two things before you go.
